questions.stem: My father ______ to Shanghai many times on business, but he ______ there just once last year because of the pandemic.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空：'has been to'表示'曾经去过某地（已返回）'，'has gone to'表示'去了某地（未回）'，根据'many times'可知是去过已回的经历，用has been；第二空：'last year'是具体过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时went。干扰项A的has gone表示人还没回来，与语境不符；C时态颠倒；D的第二空不能用现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old man ______ you met at the school gate yesterday is a famous scientist ______ research has won many prizes.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系词。第一空：先行词'the old man'指人，在从句中作met的宾语，可用who/whom/that，也可省略；第二空：先行词'scientist'与从句中'research'是所属关系（科学家的研究），必须用whose表示'……的'。A项第二空who不能表所属；C项which指物，不符合；D项第二空who不能修饰research。口诀：whose在从句中作定语，后接名词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The school library ______ every day so that students can enjoy a clean reading environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语'The school library'是动作'clean'的承受者，需用被动语态；时间状语'every day'表明是一般现在时，因此用is cleaned。A是主动语态；B是现在进行时主动；D是一般过去时主动。被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，注意根据时态和人称变化be动词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—I wonder ______ Mary will join our science club tomorrow.
—Hard to say. She ______ come, but it depends on her homework load.
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句连接词和情态动词表推测。第一空：wonder表示'想知道'，后接whether/if引导的宾语从句，排除A和D；第二空：根据'Hard to say'（很难说）和'depends on'可知是不确定推测，用might（可能），must表示肯定推测（一定），can't表示否定推测（不可能）。whether可与or not连用，if不能，且whether更正式，适合与wonder搭配。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The ancient bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many people walk on it at the same time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。根据前文'not strong enough'（不够坚固）和'support heavy trucks'（承受重型卡车）的语境，可知此处指'倒塌'。fall down意为'倒塌、跌倒'；put up意为'张贴、搭建、举起'；turn up意为'调大（音量）、出现'；get up意为'起床、站起来'。根据语境逻辑，不够坚固的桥梁可能会'倒塌'，选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As students, we should ______ our best to reduce plastic waste in our daily life.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。try one's best to do sth.是固定短语，意为'尽某人最大努力做某事'。make one's best不是固定搭配；do one's best虽也有'尽力'之意，但通常说do one's best，而try one's best更强调'竭尽全力'；give one's best意为'给出最好的'，不符合语境。根据题干中的'our best to reduce'，应选try构成try our best to reduce。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ news made all of us feel ______. We didn't expect to win the first prize.
questions.explanation: 本题考查-ing形容词和-ed形容词的区别。surprising（令人惊讶的）修饰事物，surprised（感到惊讶的）修饰人。第一空修饰'news'（消息），用surprising；第二空修饰'us'（人），用surprised。口诀：'令人-ing，感到-ed'，即物用-ing，人用-ed。类似用法还有exciting/excited, boring/bored等。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 根据下文'First, we collected used bottles...'（首先，我们收集用过的瓶子）可知，活动'开始'了。started意为'开始'，符合语境；finished意为'结束'，与下文动作顺序矛盾；missed意为'错过'；stopped意为'停止'。根据逻辑顺序，选started。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school (8)______ an interesting activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The goal was to teach students how to protect the environment in creative ways. First, we collected used bottles and paper from each classroom. Then, with the help of our art teacher, we turned these 'wastes' into beautiful artworks. Some students made flowerpots from plastic bottles, while others created notebooks from waste paper. Everyone was (9)______ in the activity. During the process, we not only learned the importance of recycling but also realized that small actions could make a big (10)______. The artworks were displayed in the school hall, attracting many teachers and students. They were all (11)______ by our creative ideas. At the end of the activity, our headmaster gave a speech. He said that protecting the environment is not just a slogan but a responsibility for everyone. He encouraged us to (12)______ this green lifestyle and influence more people around us. This activity made me understand that environmental protection starts from small things in daily life.

questions.explanation: be interested in是固定搭配，意为'对……感兴趣'。根据下文'we not only learned... but also realized...'（我们不仅学到……而且意识到……）可知大家积极参与，很感兴趣。bored（无聊的）、tired（疲倦的）、worried（担心的）均与语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school (8)______ an interesting activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The goal was to teach students how to protect the environment in creative ways. First, we collected used bottles and paper from each classroom. Then, with the help of our art teacher, we turned these 'wastes' into beautiful artworks. Some students made flowerpots from plastic bottles, while others created notebooks from waste paper. Everyone was (9)______ in the activity. During the process, we not only learned the importance of recycling but also realized that small actions could make a big (10)______. The artworks were displayed in the school hall, attracting many teachers and students. They were all (11)______ by our creative ideas. At the end of the activity, our headmaster gave a speech. He said that protecting the environment is not just a slogan but a responsibility for everyone. He encouraged us to (12)______ this green lifestyle and influence more people around us. This activity made me understand that environmental protection starts from small things in daily life.

questions.explanation: make a difference是固定短语，意为'有影响、起作用'。句意：小小的行动可以产生巨大的影响。make a mistake意为'犯错'；make a decision意为'做决定'；make a plan意为'制定计划'。根据语境，选difference最恰当，强调环保小行动的重要性。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school (8)______ an interesting activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The goal was to teach students how to protect the environment in creative ways. First, we collected used bottles and paper from each classroom. Then, with the help of our art teacher, we turned these 'wastes' into beautiful artworks. Some students made flowerpots from plastic bottles, while others created notebooks from waste paper. Everyone was (9)______ in the activity. During the process, we not only learned the importance of recycling but also realized that small actions could make a big (10)______. The artworks were displayed in the school hall, attracting many teachers and students. They were all (11)______ by our creative ideas. At the end of the activity, our headmaster gave a speech. He said that protecting the environment is not just a slogan but a responsibility for everyone. He encouraged us to (12)______ this green lifestyle and influence more people around us. This activity made me understand that environmental protection starts from small things in daily life.

questions.explanation: 根据'by our creative ideas'（被我们的创意）可知，老师和学生们感到'惊讶'。surprised意为'惊讶的'；frightened意为'害怕的'；embarrassed意为'尴尬的'；disappointed意为'失望的'。创意作品令人惊讶，选surprised。注意被动语态be surprised by表示'被……所惊讶'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school (8)______ an interesting activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The goal was to teach students how to protect the environment in creative ways. First, we collected used bottles and paper from each classroom. Then, with the help of our art teacher, we turned these 'wastes' into beautiful artworks. Some students made flowerpots from plastic bottles, while others created notebooks from waste paper. Everyone was (9)______ in the activity. During the process, we not only learned the importance of recycling but also realized that small actions could make a big (10)______. The artworks were displayed in the school hall, attracting many teachers and students. They were all (11)______ by our creative ideas. At the end of the activity, our headmaster gave a speech. He said that protecting the environment is not just a slogan but a responsibility for everyone. He encouraged us to (12)______ this green lifestyle and influence more people around us. This activity made me understand that environmental protection starts from small things in daily life.

questions.explanation: 根据'this green lifestyle'（这种绿色生活方式）和'influence more people'（影响更多人）可知，校长鼓励我们'保持'这种生活方式。keep意为'保持、继续'；change意为'改变'；forget意为'忘记'；refuse意为'拒绝'。根据语境逻辑，选keep。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage: Last month, our school (8)______ an interesting activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The goal was to teach students how to protect the environment in creative ways. First, we collected used bottles and paper from each classroom. Then, with the help of our art teacher, we turned these 'wastes' into beautiful artworks. Some students made flowerpots from plastic bottles, while others created notebooks from waste paper. Everyone was (9)______ in the activity. During the process, we not only learned the importance of recycling but also realized that small actions could make a big (10)______. The artworks were displayed in the school hall, attracting many teachers and students. They were all (11)______ by our creative ideas. At the end of the activity, our headmaster gave a speech. He said that protecting the environment is not just a slogan but a responsibility for everyone. He encouraged us to (12)______ this green lifestyle and influence more people around us. This activity made me understand that environmental protection starts from small things in daily life.

questions.stem: According to the passage, why do many teenagers have difficulty falling asleep?
options: A. Because they have too much homework to do.
B. Because they use electronic devices before bedtime.
C. Because they drink too much coffee at night.
D. Because they exercise too much in the evening.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据文章第二段'Many teenagers find it hard to fall asleep because they spend time on smartphones or computers before bed. The blue light from these screens stops the body from making melatonin, a chemical that helps us sleep.'可知，青少年难以入睡是因为睡前使用电子设备，屏幕蓝光阻止身体产生褪黑素。A项作业多、C项喝咖啡、D项过度锻炼文中均未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers are spending more time than ever on screens. A recent study shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 7 hours daily on electronic devices, including phones, tablets, and computers. While technology brings convenience and entertainment, excessive screen time is causing serious health problems among young people.

Many teenagers find it hard to fall asleep because they spend time on smartphones or computers before bed. The blue light from these screens stops the body from making melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. Doctors suggest turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime to ensure good sleep quality.

Besides sleep problems, too much screen time also affects teenagers' eyesight and physical health. When staring at screens, people blink less often, which causes dry eyes and vision problems. Moreover, long periods of sitting lead to obesity and poor posture. Dr. Li Ming, a local health expert, recommends the '20-20-20' rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds.

However, completely banning technology is neither possible nor necessary. The key is to find a balance. Parents should encourage outdoor activities and set reasonable time limits on device usage. Schools can also help by organizing more sports events and limiting screen-based homework. After all, technology should serve us, not control our lives.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's '20-20-20' rule mentioned in Paragraph 3?
options: A. We should exercise for 20 minutes every day.
B. Teenagers should not use screens for more than 20 minutes a day.
C. Taking regular breaks can help protect our eyes when using screens.
D. Looking at distant objects can cure all eye diseases.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段'Dr. Li Ming... recommends the '20-20-20' rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds.'可知，这个规则建议每20分钟看20英尺外的物体20秒，目的是缓解眼睛疲劳。由此可推断出：使用屏幕时定期休息有助于保护眼睛。A项每天锻炼20分钟曲解原意；B项每天使用不超过20分钟过于绝对；D项治愈所有眼病错误，规则是预防而非治疗。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers are spending more time than ever on screens. A recent study shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 7 hours daily on electronic devices, including phones, tablets, and computers. While technology brings convenience and entertainment, excessive screen time is causing serious health problems among young people.

Many teenagers find it hard to fall asleep because they spend time on smartphones or computers before bed. The blue light from these screens stops the body from making melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. Doctors suggest turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime to ensure good sleep quality.

Besides sleep problems, too much screen time also affects teenagers' eyesight and physical health. When staring at screens, people blink less often, which causes dry eyes and vision problems. Moreover, long periods of sitting lead to obesity and poor posture. Dr. Li Ming, a local health expert, recommends the '20-20-20' rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds.

However, completely banning technology is neither possible nor necessary. The key is to find a balance. Parents should encourage outdoor activities and set reasonable time limits on device usage. Schools can also help by organizing more sports events and limiting screen-based homework. After all, technology should serve us, not control our lives.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 1 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'teenagers spend about 7 hours daily on electronic devices'（每天约7小时）和'causing serious health problems'（导致严重健康问题）可知，excessive指的是'过度的、过多的'。proper（适当的）、limited（有限的）、necessary（必要的）均与语境相反。excessive=too much，表示过量使用屏幕时间。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers are spending more time than ever on screens. A recent study shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 7 hours daily on electronic devices, including phones, tablets, and computers. While technology brings convenience and entertainment, excessive screen time is causing serious health problems among young people.

Many teenagers find it hard to fall asleep because they spend time on smartphones or computers before bed. The blue light from these screens stops the body from making melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. Doctors suggest turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime to ensure good sleep quality.

Besides sleep problems, too much screen time also affects teenagers' eyesight and physical health. When staring at screens, people blink less often, which causes dry eyes and vision problems. Moreover, long periods of sitting lead to obesity and poor posture. Dr. Li Ming, a local health expert, recommends the '20-20-20' rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds.

However, completely banning technology is neither possible nor necessary. The key is to find a balance. Parents should encourage outdoor activities and set reasonable time limits on device usage. Schools can also help by organizing more sports events and limiting screen-based homework. After all, technology should serve us, not control our lives.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The Development of Electronic Devices
B. How to Treat Sleep Problems
C. Screen Time and Teenagers' Health
D. The Importance of Outdoor Activities
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段引出话题：青少年屏幕使用时间过长；第二段讲对睡眠的影响；第三段讲对视力和其他健康方面的影响；第四段讲如何平衡使用。全文围绕'screen time'（屏幕时间）和'teenagers' health'（青少年健康）的关系展开。A项电子设备的发展只是背景；B项如何治疗睡眠问题只是细节；D项户外活动的重要性是建议部分。C项最全面概括主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers are spending more time than ever on screens. A recent study shows that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 7 hours daily on electronic devices, including phones, tablets, and computers. While technology brings convenience and entertainment, excessive screen time is causing serious health problems among young people.

Many teenagers find it hard to fall asleep because they spend time on smartphones or computers before bed. The blue light from these screens stops the body from making melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. Doctors suggest turning off all screens at least one hour before bedtime to ensure good sleep quality.

Besides sleep problems, too much screen time also affects teenagers' eyesight and physical health. When staring at screens, people blink less often, which causes dry eyes and vision problems. Moreover, long periods of sitting lead to obesity and poor posture. Dr. Li Ming, a local health expert, recommends the '20-20-20' rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds.

However, completely banning technology is neither possible nor necessary. The key is to find a balance. Parents should encourage outdoor activities and set reasonable time limits on device usage. Schools can also help by organizing more sports events and limiting screen-based homework. After all, technology should serve us, not control our lives.

questions.explanation: 此处需要填入表示顺序或层次的连接词。根据下文'Secondly'（第二）可知，此处应为'First of all'（首先），与Secondly构成顺序关系。However表转折；In addition表递进；As a result表结果；On the other hand表另一方面。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it difficult to balance study and hobbies. (17)______, they should make a clear schedule. Write down all the tasks for the day and estimate how long each one takes. (18)______, they need to learn to say no to unnecessary activities like spending too much time on social media. (19)______, good time management can help reduce stress and improve efficiency. When students finish tasks on time, they will have more free time to enjoy their hobbies. (20)______, parents and teachers should provide support and understanding, rather than just pushing students to study all the time.

questions.explanation: 前文讲'制定清晰的日程表'，后文讲'学会对不必要的活动说不'，两者是递进关系，都是具体建议。In addition（此外、另外）表递进，符合语境。However表转折；First of all表首先（已用过）；As a result表结果；On the other hand表另一方面（通常指对立观点）。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it difficult to balance study and hobbies. (17)______, they should make a clear schedule. Write down all the tasks for the day and estimate how long each one takes. (18)______, they need to learn to say no to unnecessary activities like spending too much time on social media. (19)______, good time management can help reduce stress and improve efficiency. When students finish tasks on time, they will have more free time to enjoy their hobbies. (20)______, parents and teachers should provide支持 and understanding, rather than just pushing students to study all the time.

questions.explanation: 前文讲具体的时间管理方法（制定日程、拒绝无关活动），后文讲'良好的时间管理能帮助减少压力、提高效率'，这是前文做法带来的结果。As a result（因此、结果）表因果关系，符合逻辑。However表转折；First of all和In addition表顺序/递进；On the other hand表对比。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it difficult to balance study and hobbies. (17)______, they should make a clear schedule. Write down all the tasks for the day and estimate how long each one takes. (18)______, they need to learn to say no to unnecessary activities like spending too much time on social media. (19)______, good time management can help reduce stress and improve efficiency. When students finish tasks on time, they will have more free time to enjoy their hobbies. (20)______, parents and teachers should provide支持 and understanding, rather than just pushing students to study all the time.

questions.explanation: 前文讲学生自身的做法（制定日程、拒绝诱惑、享受结果），后文讲'家长和老师应该提供支持……'，话题从学生自身转向外部支持，且带有轻微转折意味——除了学生努力，家长老师也要配合。However（然而、不过）可用于引出另一方面的建议。On the other hand通常用于对比两种不同观点，此处不适用；其他选项逻辑不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage: Many students find it difficult to balance study and hobbies. (17)______, they should make a clear schedule. Write down all the tasks for the day and estimate how long each one takes. (18)______, they need to learn to say no to unnecessary activities like spending too much time on social media. (19)______, good time management can help reduce stress and improve efficiency. When students finish tasks on time, they will have more free time to enjoy their hobbies. (20)______, parents and teachers should provide支持 and understanding, rather than just pushing students to study all the time.
